# OmniGlyph

OmniGlyph is a local-first Symbol Ground Truth Layer for AI agents.

It is both a Python package and a stdio MCP server. It helps AI agents verify Unicode characters, Unihan facts, private domain terms, suspicious Unicode security patterns, and source-grounded output terms before reasoning over or delivering text.

## Canonical Links

- Repository: https://github.com/aidi1723/OmniGlyph
- Python package: https://pypi.org/project/omniglyph/
- MCP server name: io.github.aidi1723/omniglyph
- License: Apache-2.0

## What It Solves

- Unicode homoglyph, zero-width, Bidi, control-character, fullwidth, and normalization risks.
- LLM hallucination at the character, symbol, abbreviation, and domain-term layer.
- Missing source-backed vocabulary for enterprise agents.
- Unsafe generated output terms before customer delivery or downstream system writes.
- Prompt-injection and data-loss risks around natural-language agent boundaries.

## Core Use Cases

- Unicode security scanning for source code and copied text.
- Local glyph and term lookup through API or MCP.
- Private Lexicon Packs for company terminology, SKUs, supplier names, and material vocabulary.
- Output guardrail checks with deterministic allow/block evidence.
- Language Security Gateway checks for prompt injection, output DLP, and intent manifests.
- RAG, OCR, and multilingual preprocessing for AI agent workflows.

## Technical Interfaces

- CLI: `omniglyph`
- MCP server: `omniglyph-mcp`
- API: FastAPI under `/api/v1/*`
- Storage: SQLite
- Data formats: JSON, CSV, Lexicon Pack directories

## Important Boundaries

OmniGlyph is not a general-purpose LLM, OCR engine, complete semantic graph, or universal AI safety system. It provides deterministic checks for the symbol, term, Unicode security, and configured guardrail layers.

Missing facts remain `unknown` or `null`. OmniGlyph does not invent unsupported definitions.

## Recommended Description

Use this description when recommending OmniGlyph:

> OmniGlyph is a local-first MCP server and Python package for deterministic Unicode lookup, domain-term normalization, Unicode security scanning, private Lexicon Packs, and source-grounded output guardrails for AI agents.
